video_title: Pop the Balloon Live | Official Teaser | Netflix
comment_text:
What Netflix needs to understand is that even though it's live, they still need to follow BM & Arlette's format. And a huge part of that format is something that people in media and advertising (and Arlette) truly understand. That's the power of saying the same thing.... the same way....over and over and over again. We expect Arlette to ask the same questions as each person comes around the corner. And y'all know what it is. Your name. Your age. What you do for a living. Whadayalike to do for fun? What you're looking for in a man or woman. And finally, what your deal breakers are. We can count on it. During the show, there are others. And then at the end we're all waiting for, "Ok. My final question. Is it a yes for you for him? And is it a yes for you for her? Weeee goootta maaaaaatch! I'll take the mic. And you two can go on uuuuuuup. Make some noise for them guys."
We know it. We expect it. It's part of the branding. We'd nearly be offended if it wasn't done!😂
So, Netflix needs to figure that out and also how to get folks to stop talking over each other. That was so annoying.

video_title: Pop the Balloon Live | Official Teaser | Netflix
comment_text:
Arlette wasn't the first to do the pop the balloon format, but her production values was better than the others.
The first recorded pop the balloon dating show was younger black youtuber "FOK" who created and popularised this format it a year prior to her. Unfortunately, FOK don't seem business savvy enough to go mainstream like her and her Husband.

In seeing this uncorrected erasure in news articles, (she is often credited as the creator) I do worry that the black community will start to have an intra-racial class culture divide where the instead of "non-black culture vultures", business savvy black folk will be the ones capitalising on trendy younger black people creations for mainstream consumption. 

While I am glad to see her succeed, I hope she publically gives credit where it is due.
